messages.title: AI agent handoff quality review · Resolved
messages.body:
# AI agent handoff quality review · Resolved
> the support agent marked a billing ticket as solved but customer sentiment stayed negative

## What changed
- Human review confirmed the answer and the new guardrail reduced risky closures in the sample.
- Resolution confidence was 0.86, while sentiment score remained below the escalation threshold.
- The customer received a follow-up answer with the correct invoice reference.

## Handling
1. Sent the ticket to manual review and attached the model trace.
2. Added a guardrail for billing replies when sentiment stays negative.
3. Sample the next 50 billing tickets before restoring full automation.

## Next step
- Owner: Support automation lead
- Expected time: next stable sample window

messages.body:
# AI agent handoff quality review · Investigating
> the support agent marked a billing ticket as solved but customer sentiment stayed negative

## What changed
- One enterprise customer waited 14 minutes longer for a human confirmation.
- Resolution confidence was 0.86, while sentiment score remained below the escalation threshold.
- The customer received a follow-up answer with the correct invoice reference.

## Handling
1. Sent the ticket to manual review and attached the model trace.
2. Added a guardrail for billing replies when sentiment stays negative.
3. Sample the next 50 billing tickets before restoring full automation.

## Next step
- Owner: Support automation lead
- Expected time: next stable sample window

messages.title: Unusual login retry pattern · Resolved
messages.body:
# Unusual login retry pattern · Resolved
> customer login retries clustered around three network ranges

## What changed
- The retry rate fell back to baseline and no new high-risk session appeared for 30 minutes.
- Failed login rate rose to 11.8% and normalized after adaptive challenges were enabled.
- No successful account takeover signal was found in the sample.

## Handling
1. Enabled stricter risk challenges for the affected ranges.
2. Shared the timeline with customer trust for proactive support notes.
3. Review whether the retry pattern matches a known credential stuffing list.

## Next step
- Owner: Trust operations
- Expected time: next stable sample window

messages.body:
# Unusual login retry pattern · Investigating
> customer login retries clustered around three network ranges

## What changed
- Password reset and account center pages saw brief extra friction.
- Failed login rate rose to 11.8% and normalized after adaptive challenges were enabled.
- No successful account takeover signal was found in the sample.

## Handling
1. Enabled stricter risk challenges for the affected ranges.
2. Shared the timeline with customer trust for proactive support notes.
3. Review whether the retry pattern matches a known credential stuffing list.

## Next step
- Owner: Trust operations
- Expected time: next stable sample window

messages.title: Payment API latency spike · Resolved
messages.body:
# Payment API latency spike · Resolved
> checkout requests in East China slowed for business users

## What changed
- Latency returned below the 300 ms target and the replica lag stayed under 5 s for 20 minutes.
- The p95 latency peaked at 1.9 s, then started to fall after replica traffic was reduced.
- No data loss was detected; three retries were automatically recovered.

## Handling
1. Pinned the slow query fingerprint and removed the newest read replica from rotation.
2. Raised database connection pool telemetry to one-minute granularity.
3. Keep the incident open until the next billing batch completes without retry growth.

## Next step
- Owner: SRE on call
- Expected time: next stable sample window

messages.title: Payment API latency spike · Investigating
messages.body:
# Payment API latency spike · Investigating
> checkout requests in East China slowed for business users

## What changed
- Checkout confirmation was delayed for 3.8% of requests in the Shanghai region.
- The p95 latency peaked at 1.9 s, then started to fall after replica traffic was reduced.
- No data loss was detected; three retries were automatically recovered.

## Handling
1. Pinned the slow query fingerprint and removed the newest read replica from rotation.
2. Raised database connection pool telemetry to one-minute granularity.
3. Keep the incident open until the next billing batch completes without retry growth.

## Next step
- Owner: SRE on call
- Expected time: next stable sample window

messages.title: Kitchen leak sensor triggered · Resolved
messages.body:
# Kitchen leak sensor triggered · Resolved
> the floor sensor reported continuous moisture near the sink cabinet

## What changed
- The moisture curve stayed flat for 25 minutes and the valve was reopened in supervised mode.
- Humidity fell from 72% to 38% after the valve closed and the ventilation fan started.
- One household routine was paused; manual override remains available in the app.

## Handling
1. Validated that the sensor was not reporting a battery-low false positive.
2. Kept the valve closed while the camera snapshot was reviewed.
3. Ask the resident to check the cabinet joint before reopening the branch.

## Next step
- Owner: Home automation owner
- Expected time: next stable sample window

messages.title: Possible duplicate supplier invoice · Resolved
messages.body:
# Possible duplicate supplier invoice · Resolved
> two invoices for the same purchase order arrived through different approval paths

## What changed
- The second invoice was confirmed as a resubmission and the original payment schedule was restored.
- The duplicate score reached 0.93 because amount, supplier tax number and delivery note matched.
- The finance team can still release the valid invoice after secondary approval.

## Handling
1. Locked both invoice records and attached the supplier delivery note.
2. Asked procurement to confirm which invoice number should remain active.
3. Keep the cash-flow forecast marked as tentative until the hold is cleared.

## Next step
- Owner: Finance controller
- Expected time: next stable sample window

messages.title: Kitchen leak sensor triggered · Investigating
messages.body:
# Kitchen leak sensor triggered · Investigating
> the floor sensor reported continuous moisture near the sink cabinet

## What changed
- The smart valve closed the cold-water branch in 8 seconds and prevented spread beyond the cabinet.
- Humidity fell from 72% to 38% after the valve closed and the ventilation fan started.
- One household routine was paused; manual override remains available in the app.

## Handling
1. Validated that the sensor was not reporting a battery-low false positive.
2. Kept the valve closed while the camera snapshot was reviewed.
3. Ask the resident to check the cabinet joint before reopening the branch.

## Next step
- Owner: Home automation owner
- Expected time: next stable sample window

messages.title: Possible duplicate supplier invoice · Investigating
messages.body:
# Possible duplicate supplier invoice · Investigating
> two invoices for the same purchase order arrived through different approval paths

## What changed
- A scheduled payment worth 18,420.00 CNY was held before bank submission.
- The duplicate score reached 0.93 because amount, supplier tax number and delivery note matched.
- The finance team can still release the valid invoice after secondary approval.

## Handling
1. Locked both invoice records and attached the supplier delivery note.
2. Asked procurement to confirm which invoice number should remain active.
3. Keep the cash-flow forecast marked as tentative until the hold is cleared.

## Next step
- Owner: Finance controller
- Expected time: next stable sample window
